**PROJECT REQUIREMENTS**

**InvoiceFlow**

*AI-Powered Invoice Processing & Approval System*

| Stack | .NET 10 Web API   |   Angular 18   |   Microsoft Azure |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Architecture** | Event-Driven  ·  CQRS with MediatR  ·  Clean Architecture |
| **Azure Cost** | \~$1–3 / month on Student Subscription ($100 credit) |
| **Type** | Portfolio / Learning Project  —  Backend Heavy |

 

# **1\. Overview**

InvoiceFlow automates the end-to-end lifecycle of vendor invoice processing. Vendors upload invoices (PDF or image); Azure AI Document Intelligence extracts all key fields automatically; a configurable approval chain routes the invoice to the right people; every action is logged in an immutable audit trail.

 

| Problem | Solution |
| :---- | :---- |
| Manual data entry of invoice fields | AI extracts all fields automatically |
| Approvals tracked over email | Structured multi-step digital workflow |
| No audit trail on who approved what | Immutable audit log on every action |
| Duplicate invoices paid multiple times | Automated duplicate detection |

 

# **2\. Azure Services Used**

| Service | Free Tier | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| AI Document Intelligence | 500 pages / month | Prebuilt invoice model — extracts vendor, amount, date, line items, confidence score. Zero training required. |
| Azure Blob Storage | 5 GB, 20K ops | Store uploaded invoice PDFs. Generate SAS URLs for secure preview. |
| Azure Service Bus | \~$0.10 / month | Async message queue — decouples API from AI extraction and notifications. |
| Azure Functions | 1M executions | Process extraction jobs, run daily approval reminders, dispatch notifications. |
| Azure SQL Database | Free serverless | Store invoices, approval workflows, audit logs, users, and vendors. |
| Application Insights | 5 GB / month | Distributed tracing, custom metrics, exception logging. |
| Key Vault | 10K ops / month | Store all secrets and connection strings — nothing in code. |
| App Service (F1) | Free | Host the .NET 10 Web API on Azure. |
| SignalR Service | Free (20 conns) | Real-time in-app notifications on extraction and approval events. |

 

# **3\. Key Features**

## **Invoice Management**

•       Upload PDF / PNG / JPG invoices up to 10 MB — stored in Blob Storage with GUID-based paths

•       Azure AI Document Intelligence extracts: vendor, invoice number, date, due date, line items, totals, currency

•       Per-field confidence scores — fields below 0.70 flagged for manual correction in the UI

•       Duplicate detection: same vendor \+ invoice number \+ amount within 30 days → 409 Conflict

•       Status tracking: Uploaded → Extracting → Extracted → PendingApproval → InApproval → Approved / Rejected

 

## **Approval Workflow Engine**

| Amount | Approval Chain | Steps |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Up to ₹50,000 | Manager only | 1 |
| ₹50K – ₹5,00,000 | Manager \+ Finance Head | 2 — sequential |
| Above ₹5,00,000 | Manager \+ Finance \+ CFO | 3 — sequential |

 

•       Approve (optional comment)  /  Reject (mandatory comment)  /  Delegate to another user

•       Azure Function timer runs daily — reminders at 48 h, escalation to manager at 72 h

 

## **Other Features**

•       Vendor registry — whitelist / blacklist, invoice history, spend summary

•       Role-based access: Vendor, Employee, Manager, Finance Head, CFO, Admin

•       Analytics dashboard — KPI cards, monthly spend trends, SLA breach tracker

•       Full immutable audit log — every status change, approval decision, and admin action

•       CSV export of filtered audit log

 

# **4\. API Endpoints (Summary)**

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **POST** | /api/invoices/upload | Upload invoice — returns 202 Accepted \+ invoice ID |
| **GET** | /api/invoices/{id}/status | Poll extraction status |
| **PUT** | /api/invoices/{id}/extracted-data | Manually correct low-confidence AI-extracted fields |
| **GET** | /api/invoices/{id}/blob-url | Generate 60-min SAS URL for PDF preview |
| **POST** | /api/approvals/{stepId}/approve | Approve workflow step |
| **POST** | /api/approvals/{stepId}/reject | Reject workflow step (comment mandatory) |
| **POST** | /api/approvals/{stepId}/delegate | Delegate step to another eligible user |
| **GET** | /api/analytics/summary | KPI counts, avg extraction time, avg approval time |
| **GET** | /api/admin/audit-logs | Searchable audit log with CSV export |

 

# **5\. Architecture Patterns**

| Pattern | How it is used |
| :---- | :---- |
| Event-Driven | API publishes to Service Bus and returns 202 immediately. Azure Functions handle AI extraction and notifications asynchronously — API never blocks. |
| CQRS \+ MediatR | All operations are Commands (write) or Queries (read) dispatched through MediatR. Pipeline Behaviours handle validation and duplicate detection globally. |
| Clean Architecture | API → Application → Domain → Infrastructure layers. Azure SDK clients are injected via interfaces — fully mockable for unit tests. |
| JWT \+ Policy Auth | Six roles enforced via ASP.NET Core policy-based authorization. Vendors can only access their own data. |
| Azure Key Vault | All secrets and connection strings at runtime from Key Vault — nothing stored in config files or source code. |

 

# **6\. Build Timeline**

| Week | Focus | Deliverables |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | Foundation | Azure SQL schema, EF Core, JWT auth, Blob Storage upload, local dev setup |
| **2** | AI Core | Azure Functions, Service Bus, Document Intelligence call, status polling |
| **3** | Approval Engine | Workflow tables, routing rules, approve/reject/delegate, reminder timer |
| **4** | Angular Frontend | Upload UI, approver dashboard, PDF preview, SignalR notifications |
| **5** | Analytics & Admin | KPI dashboard, spend charts, vendor management, audit log viewer, CSV export |
| **6** | Polish & Deploy | App Insights metrics, health checks, Key Vault wiring, deploy to Azure |

 

![][image1]  
Here's the complete system design. I'll break it into three visual sections — architecture, invoice lifecycle flow, and the database schema — with full feature and API breakdowns in between.

\---

\#\# Project overview

The project is called \*InvoiceFlow\* — an AI-powered invoice processing and multi-step approval system. The core idea is: any vendor uploads an invoice (PDF or image), the system extracts structured data using Azure AI Document Intelligence, validates it, routes it through a configurable approval chain, and maintains a full audit trail. Everything is event-driven via Azure Service Bus so the API never blocks.

\---

\#\# System architecture

This is the high-level structural view — what lives where and how layers communicate.---

\#\# Invoice lifecycle — end to end

This is the most important flow to understand deeply. Every Azure service earns its place in a specific step.Click any step above to ask a deep-dive question about it. Now let's look at the database schema and API structure.

\---

\#\# Database schema (ERD)---

\#\# Full feature breakdown---

\#\# API endpoint map

Here's the complete REST surface organized by domain:

\*Invoices\*  
\- POST /api/invoices/upload — multipart upload, returns 202 \+ job ID  
\- GET /api/invoices — paginated list (filtered by role)  
\- GET /api/invoices/{id} — full invoice detail \+ extraction result  
\- GET /api/invoices/{id}/status — polling endpoint for extraction progress  
\- PUT /api/invoices/{id}/extracted-data — manual correction of AI-extracted fields  
\- GET /api/invoices/{id}/audit — full audit trail for an invoice  
\- GET /api/invoices/{id}/blob-url — generate short-lived SAS URL for PDF preview

\*Approvals\*  
\- GET /api/approvals/pending — invoices awaiting my approval  
\- POST /api/approvals/{stepId}/approve — approve with optional comment  
\- POST /api/approvals/{stepId}/reject — reject with mandatory comment  
\- POST /api/approvals/{stepId}/delegate — delegate to another user  
\- GET /api/approvals/{invoiceId}/workflow — full workflow chain \+ status

\*Vendors\*  
\- GET /api/vendors — list all vendors  
\- POST /api/vendors — register new vendor  
\- PATCH /api/vendors/{id}/whitelist — toggle whitelist status

\*Analytics\*  
\- GET /api/analytics/summary — counts by status, avg approval time  
\- GET /api/analytics/spend — spend aggregated by month/vendor/dept  
\- GET /api/analytics/sla — invoices approaching or breaching SLA

\*Admin\*  
\- GET /api/admin/users — all users with roles  
\- PATCH /api/admin/users/{id}/role — update user role  
\- GET /api/admin/approval-rules — get configured amount thresholds  
\- PUT /api/admin/approval-rules — update amount thresholds  
\- GET /api/admin/audit-logs — searchable system-wide audit log

\---

\#\# Project folder structure

InvoiceFlow/  
├── src/  
│   ├── InvoiceFlow.API/             ← .NET 10 Web API  
│   │   ├── Controllers/  
│   │   ├── Middleware/              ← Global error handler, request logging  
│   │   └── Program.cs  
│   ├── InvoiceFlow.Application/     ← CQRS commands, queries, validators  
│   │   ├── Invoices/  
│   │   │   ├── Commands/  
│   │   │   └── Queries/  
│   │   ├── Approvals/  
│   │   └── Common/  
│   ├── InvoiceFlow.Domain/          ← Entities, enums, domain events  
│   ├── InvoiceFlow.Infrastructure/  ← EF Core, Azure SDK integrations  
│   │   ├── Persistence/  
│   │   ├── AzureStorage/  
│   │   ├── ServiceBus/  
│   │   └── DocumentIntelligence/  
│   ├── InvoiceFlow.Functions/       ← Azure Functions project  
│   │   ├── ExtractionProcessor.cs  
│   │   ├── ApprovalReminderTimer.cs  
│   │   └── NotificationDispatcher.cs  
│   └── InvoiceFlow.Angular/         ← Angular 18 frontend  
│       ├── src/app/  
│       │   ├── invoices/  
│       │   ├── approvals/  
│       │   ├── analytics/  
│       │   └── admin/  
│       └── environments/  
└── tests/  
    ├── InvoiceFlow.UnitTests/  
    └── InvoiceFlow.IntegrationTests/

\---

\#\# Suggested build order (4–6 weeks)

\*Week 1\* — Foundation: Azure SQL schema, EF Core setup, JWT auth, basic upload API, Blob Storage integration.

\*Week 2\* — AI core: Azure Functions project, Service Bus integration, AI Document Intelligence call, extraction result storage, status polling endpoint.

\*Week 3\* — Approval engine: workflow table, approval routing rules, step-by-step decision flow, reminder timer function.

\*Week 4\* — Angular frontend: upload UI, approver dashboard, PDF preview with SAS URL, status tracking.

\*Week 5\* — Analytics \+ admin: dashboard charts, vendor management, audit log viewer, CSV export.

\*Week 6\* — Polish: App Insights telemetry, health checks, Key Vault for secrets, integration tests, Swagger documentation.

\---

Click any "Show me the code" button above to get the actual implementation code for that specific feature. The most impactful ones to implement first are the Blob Storage upload, the AI Document Intelligence call, and the Service Bus event flow — those three together already tell an impressive story in an interview.
